criteria.label: Use `psutil` (or `os` / dashboards) to check RSS before reaching for heavier profilers.
Take tracemalloc snapshots and read the top-N allocations by file/line.
criteria.example: `snap.statistics('lineno')[:15]` to triage the largest allocation sites.

criteria.label: Use `snapshot.compare_to` (or `memray --leaks`) to identify leak suspects via deltas.
criteria.example: Run the suspect op 10× between snapshots; the diff names the line that's growing.

criteria.label: Pick the right representation for big numeric data (numpy / arrow / quantised / mmap).
criteria.example: 1M × 768-dim embeddings → numpy fp32 (3 GB) or fp16 (1.5 GB); mmap FAISS for >RAM indices.

criteria.label: Bound caches and shared structures (`maxsize`, TTLCache).
Diagnose mixed Python/C memory pressure by combining tracemalloc with C-side stats.
criteria.example: RSS climbing but Python heap flat → check `numpy_array.nbytes`, FAISS index info, GPU memory.
